data.title: Trump's New Tariffs Could Send the UK Spiraling into a Recession
data.description:
President Trump will impose an additional 10% tariff on eight countries, including the United Kingdom, starting on February 1. Afterward, the rate will rise to 25% on June 1. The tariffs will remain in place until the U.S. secures a deal to acquire Greenland.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That could send the UK into a recession, according to Capital Economics. The firm estimates that a 25% tariff on top of the country's existing 10% tariff will decrease its gross domestic product (GDP) by 0.75%, equivalent to a $25.15 billion hit. "With the UK economy currently growing by 0.2-0.3pc a quarter, if this hit came all at once it could trigger a recession," said Capital Economics Chief UK Economist Paul Dales.

Starmer Calls for 'Calm Discussion' on Greenland Dispute

Unlike several other European leaders, UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has talked down the prospect of retaliatory tariffs and instead proposed a negotiation process. "I think that this can be resolved, and should be resolved, through calm discussion," he said. Starmer added that imposing tariffs is the wrong approach when addressing differences within an alliance and that the people of Greenland should have control over their future.

European Union leaders are scheduled to meet this Thursday for an emergency summit to discuss their options, which include reviving a $103 billion tariff package on U.S. goods that was shelved last year after the two sides reached a trade agreement.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 'Get Ready for the Next Upswing,' Says Analyst About Ethereum (ETH)
data.description:
2025 ended in disappointing fashion for Bitcoin (BTC-USD) investors. While many prognosticators expected its price to peak above $150,000, Bitcoin's rally stalled and the cryptocurrency actually saw out the year 6% into the red. Of course, as is always the case, Bitcoin dragged down with it all other tokens.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

However, looking specifically at the case of the second biggest crypto Ethereum (ETH-USD), Standard Chartered's Head of Digital Assets Research, Geoff Kendrick, thinks its prospects have gotten better, and as such, he eventually sees the ETH-BTC price making its way back to its 2021 highs of around 0.08.

Kendrick believes there are several factors that should support outperformance from Ethereum. For one, although ETF and digital asset treasury (DAT) inflows have slowed across the sector, ongoing accumulation by Bitmine Immersion Technologies, the largest ETH-focused DAT, provides a relative tailwind for Ethereum.

The DAT accumulation of digital assets has largely stalled following the late-2025 collapse in mNAVs (market net asset value), a valuation metric that compares a company's market capitalization with the value of the digital assets it holds. Elevated mNAVs above 1.0 had previously enabled DATs to effectively convert $1.00 into $1.50, but several major DATs now trade below 1.0, including Strategy and SharpLink Gaming. These valuations saw little improvement even after MSCI confirmed on January 6 that DATs would not be excluded from its indices, following the earlier October 2025 review announcement.

In contrast, Bitmine has maintained an mNAV of roughly 1.1–1.2, allowing it to continue accumulating ETH at a "reasonably fast pace." Bitmine now holds 3.4% of ETH in circulation and remains on track to reach its stated 5% target by late Q1 or early Q2. "Against this backdrop," Kendrick says, "DAT buying of ETH appears more sustainable than DAT buying of BTC.

Second, Kendrick is still "very bullish" on the outlook for stablecoins, RWAs (real-world assets) and DeFi (decentralised finance), segments where Ethereum continues to hold a dominant position. By the end of 2028, Kendrick expects both stablecoins and RWAs to reach market caps of $2 trillion, rising from current levels of $308 billion for stablecoins (according to Artemis), and $40 billion for RWAs (according to The Block).

Lastly, In June 2025, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin outlined plans to scale ETH's layer 1 throughput by 10x over the following two to three years, a plan that so far "appears to be working," with Kendrick's analysis showing that "higher throughput translates into higher market cap."

Despite Kendrick's more constructive view on ETH relative to BTC, the analyst has reduced his ETH-USD forecasts for 2026–28 to account for BTC's weakness. That said, he also increased his ETH-USD forecast for end-2029 and expects the price to reach $40,000 by the end of 2030.
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: 2026's Big Air Race: Does Boeing (NYSE:BA) Come Out Ahead of Airbus?
data.description:
One thing we can say about 2025 in aerospace was that it was extremely competitive. Aerospace stock Boeing (BA) managed to do quite well with larger aircraft, while its immediate rival, Airbus (EADSY) managed to come out on top with smaller commercial aircraft. But what about 2026? We are barely three weeks into the new year, and it is already shaping up to be fundamentally different from 2025. Who is likely to come out on top this year? The answer might surprise you.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Boeing: A Come-From-Behind…Win?

There is a lot to like about Boeing's 2026. The clear point in Boeing's favor here is the labor contracts. 2024 saw the union in Washington go on strike. 2025, meanwhile, saw IAM District 837 in St. Louis go on strike from late August to November. With both strikes resolved–and a third with the SPEEA also resolved to the tentative agreement level just days ago–this means Boeing's labor troubles are likely to be quiet for 2026.

It also does not hurt that the 737 MAX 10 is closer than ever to certification. Getting that plane into the field this year would be a huge game-changer, assuming it can get through the certification process. Granted, that is a long assumption; the 737 MAX 10's certification process has been delayed before, and could easily be delayed again. It remains, however, that getting it in play in 2026 would turn things around quite well.

However, Boeing will see a lot of downsides to 2026. The first is that the boost of President Trump's trade deal tour that Boeing got in 2025 will not be happening again this year. While this means fewer orders, the good news is that the process of selling a plane means that Boeing will make money for filling orders, which it has plenty of on hand.

Boeing also has a production cap from the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to consider. The cap has been raised once, and there are good signs that it will be raised at least once more before the end of 2026. Given that Boeing has cooperated with the FAA in every particular since the last time the cap was raised, and no significant issues have arisen in the meantime, the idea that the FAA would again raise the cap is logically valid. It is possible that the cap may not be raised, and either way, the cap is still there and will be there for the foreseeable future.

Airbus labors under no such formal, regulatory cap.

Airbus: Building on Its Successes

Airbus' year looks pretty bright by comparison. While it lacks some of Boeing's advantages, it also lacks several of Boeing's drawbacks.

Airbus is already running at capacity right now. The reports suggest that Airbus is pretty much maxed out in terms of production right now, so it is producing at a rate that is likely to surpass Boeing. However, that also suggests that any problem at Airbus—any supply chain hiccup, any mechanical breakdown, or the like—is likely to hit Airbus and knock it off this perch. The same goes for any issues with its engine supplier, which will hit Airbus unusually hard.

Airbus has an advantage in public perception as well. Boeing is perhaps best known in the field, but Boeing is also connected to being the aircraft maker that recently had a lot of mechanical failures. Airbus can comfortably work from that position of strength, but again, if anything goes wrong for Airbus, it loses that advantage. If something happens to Boeing, Boeing supporters would note that it can bounce back faster, simply by referring to everything it has already done to fix things from the last failure.

Airbus also, at last report, has a larger backlog to work through than Boeing does. This means that Airbus can put production capacity that is superior to Boeing's right now to work harder, for longer. This should ultimately let it pull out ahead.

Basically, Airbus is on top of the game right now, but Boeing can better absorb any kind of failure or downturn. Airbus, should it end up in a similar position, will be hit as hard as Boeing was the first time these issues came to light.

So Who Wins?

Granted, your definition of "win" may be different from anyone else's, including my own. But it looks like this race will be Airbus' to lose this year. Airbus has a greater backlog and no limits on production capacity save its own. Boeing is still fighting to win back trust and capability from before the series of malfunctions hit. So while Boeing will put on a good showing this year, Airbus is likely to come out on top.

Which Stock Comes Out on Top?

When comparing the two stocks, Boeing is rated a Strong Buy with a 4.04% upside potential on a $257.69 average price target. Meanwhile, Airbus offers a 170.01% upside potential against an average price target of $170.46 per share, rated a Moderate Buy.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: OpenAI Says 2026 Will Be a Turning Point for Practical AI Adoption
data.description:
Microsoft-backed (MSFT) AI firm OpenAI wants 2026 to be the year that its artificial intelligence tools move from impressive demos to everyday use, according to a new blog post from CFO Sarah Friar. More specifically, she said that the main goal is to close the gap between what AI is capable of and how people, companies, and governments actually use it day-to-day. Friar pointed to areas like healthcare, scientific research, and enterprise software as places where better AI can quickly lead to better results.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

She also explained that OpenAI's revenue growth is closely tied to how much computing power it can access. In fact, Friar pointed out that OpenAI's compute capacity increased from about 0.2 gigawatts in 2023 to roughly 1.9 gigawatts in 2025, while its annual revenue run rate rose from $2 billion to more than $20 billion over the same period. In her view, this shows that more infrastructure directly leads to faster adoption and higher revenue.

As a result, OpenAI has entered major infrastructure partnerships, including a widely discussed agreement with chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) that was announced last September. Indeed, Nvidia said that it would support OpenAI with up to $100 billion in AI systems with at least 10 gigawatts of capacity, which is an amount comparable to the power used by millions of U.S. homes. However, Nvidia later warned investors that there is no guarantee the deal will move forward. Nevertheless, Friar noted that OpenAI now works with multiple compute providers, thereby allowing it to plan and scale more reliably.

Is MSFT Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 32 Buys and two Holds assigned in the last three months. Furthermore, the average MSFT price target of $631.36 per share implies 37.3% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Trump Doesn't Rule Out Using Force to Acquire Greenland
data.description:
Global geopolitical tensions are ramping higher after President Trump announced a 10% tariff on eight European countries that will remain in place until the U.S. secures a deal to purchase Greenland. Trump didn't do much to ease the pressure after he responded with "no comment," when asked if he would use force to acquire the self-governing territory.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The countries facing tariffs include Germany, the United Kingdom, Norway, and Denmark, which governs Greenland under a self-rule arrangement.

EU Considers Retaliation Options After Trump's Greenland Tariffs

Earlier today, a letter written by Trump to Norwegian Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Støre was publicized. The letter noted that Trump no longer has "an obligation to think purely of Peace" because he did not receive the Nobel Peace Prize for "having stopped 8 Wars."

The European Union is currently considering several options, such as a $103 billion tariff package on U.S. goods and the anti-coercion instrument (ACI). The ACI was formed in 2023 and allows the EU to impose retaliatory tariffs and taxes on foreign companies in response to coercive trade practices.

data.title: New Data Shows that Meta's Threads Has Overtaken Musk's X in Daily Mobile Usage
data.description:
New data from market intelligence firm Similarweb (SMWB) suggests that Meta's (META) social media platform Threads has overtaken Elon Musk's X in daily mobile usage. While X continues to lead in web usage, Threads' iOS and Android apps have steadily gained traction over the past several months. More specifically, as of January 7, 2026, Threads recorded about 141.5 million daily active mobile users, compared with roughly 125 million for X.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Importantly, this trend does not appear to be driven by short-term controversy surrounding X. Although recent issues involving X's AI tool Grok and the creation of non-consensual deepfake images have drawn regulatory scrutiny in regions like California, the UK, and the EU, Similarweb's data shows that Threads' growth started before those events. In addition, other platforms, such as Bluesky, have seen temporary download spikes, but Threads' usage gains look more durable.

Interestingly, much of Threads' momentum is due to Meta's ecosystem advantage and aggressive product development. Indeed, the app benefits from being constantly promoted across Facebook and Instagram, along with a strong focus on creators, and a steady rollout of new features, such as communities, direct messages, long-form posts, and experimental tools like games. Moreover, Meta has previously disclosed that Threads surpassed 400 million monthly active users by August 2025.

What Is the Price Target for Meta?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on META stock based on 37 Buys, six Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average META price target of $823.63 per share implies 32.8% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Here's Why Iren Stock Rallied 11%
data.description:
Shares in Bitcoin miner and data center operator Iren Limited (IREN) closed Friday's session 11.43% stronger. The rally came as AI chip stocks cheered the blockbuster fourth-quarter results posted by TSMC (TSM), the world's largest contract chip manufacturer.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During the quarter, TSMC grew its revenue by 26% to $33.73 billion. And more importantly, the chip foundry stated plans to spend between $52 billion and $56 billion in 2026, especially on advanced technologies.

Sydney-based Iren, alongside rivals such as CoreWeave (CRWV) in the U.S. and Nebius (NBIS) in the Netherlands, generates and provides scarce AI cloud computing capacity to hyperscalers such as Microsoft (MSFT) and Meta (META).

They are, therefore, traded as a proxy for the AI boom. For instance, shares in these AI-focused "neocloud" companies surged in November after AI chip leader Nvidia (NVDA) smashed Wall Street estimates for its third-quarter results.

Iren Kicks Off 'Transformative' Year

Despite suffering a rut in the final weeks of 2025 following its Q1 2026 earnings results, Iren shares surged over 300% in the year, fueled by continued hype for AI infrastructure companies. Since the start of this year, the stock has rallied a further 53%.

H.C. Wainwright analyst Mike Colonnese has contended that Iren is "set for a transformative year." Colonnese pointed to continued heavy spending on AI infrastructure by hyperscalers.

The five-star analyst argued that Iren's recent $9.7 billion five-year deal with Microsoft is yet to reflect in its share price. He believes the Australian company could generate $1.94 billion in annual recurring revenue once the deal is fully ramped this year.

On the contrary, the team at banking titan Goldman Sachs (GS) recently slapped a Hold rating on Iren. The team believes that Iren is seeing "hypergrowth" but argued that the growth pipeline for the neocloud company beyond the Microsoft deal raises some doubt.

Is IREN Stock a Good Buy?

Across Wall Street, Iren Limited's shares remain a Moderate Buy based on the analyst consensus rating. This breaks down to nine Buys, two Holds, and one Sell issued by 12 analysts over the past three months.

Nonetheless, the average IREN price target of $82.80 suggests over 43% growth potential.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) Develops 24/7 Stock Trading for the NYSE
data.description:
Intercontinental Exchange (ICE), which owns and operates the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), has developed a new blockchain platform that will allow investors to trade stocks around the clock.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Management at Intercontinental Exchange says they're seeking regulatory approval for a "tokenized securities platform" that will enable investors to trade stocks and fractional shares 24 hours a day, seven days a week.

Intercontinental Exchange is working with financial institutions such as Bank of New York Mellon (BK) and Citigroup (C) to support tokenized deposits and operate the 24/7 trading on the New York Stock Exchange. However, the tokenized platform still requires approval from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) before it can be formally launched.

Tokenization Explained

Tokenization is when ownership rights to a stock or other security are digitized so that they can be traded nonstop. ICE is the latest stock exchange to move toward around-the-clock trading. Last September, the NYSE's main rival Nasdaq (NDAQ) said that it had asked the SEC to allow investors to trade tokenized versions of stocks on its exchange.

Elsewhere, the online trading platform Robinhood Markets (HOOD) and cryptocurrency exchanges Coinbase (COIN) and Gemini (GEMI) have also proposed launching tokenized versions of assets such as stocks, bonds, and gold. The companies say they're responding to demand from investors who increasingly want the ability to trade securities at all hours.

Is ICE Stock a Buy?

Intercontinental Exchange's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among nine Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on eight Buy and one Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average ICE price target of $191.44 implies 10% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: U.S. GDP Could Blast Past Estimates This Year, Says Goldman Sachs
data.description:
Goldman Sachs (GS) expects U.S. gross domestic product (GDP) to grow by 2.5% during the fourth quarter of 2026, well above the consensus economist estimate of 2.1%. It expects 2.8% growth for the full year.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A major factor behind the firm's optimistic estimate is for tariff headwinds to ease alongside lower taxes for businesses and consumers from the One Big Beautiful Bill. "Tax cuts, real wage gains, and rising wealth should sustain solid consumer spending growth," wrote Goldman Chief U.S. Economist David Mericle in an outlook report.

AI to Drive Productivity Growth in 2026, Says Goldman

The firm predicts that an increase in productivity, partly driven by AI, will account for a greater proportion of GDP expansion. On the other hand, immigration policies will reduce the labor supply's contribution to GDP growth.

However, Goldman points out a softening labor market as a major risk. The unemployment rate was 4.4% in December, falling from 4.5% in November. November's rate was revised lower from 4.6%, which marked the highest level since September 2021.
